chapter_title.en: Chapter on the Inheritance of the Killer, Who Inherits From Blood Money and Who Does Not

text: 5683 - رَوَى صَفْوَانُ بْنُ يَحْيَى عَنِ اِبْنِ أَبِي عُمَيْرٍ عَنْ جَمِيلٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ قَتَلَ أَبَاهُ قَالَ "لاَ يَرِثُهُ وَ إِنْ كَانَ لِلْقَاتِلِ اِبْنٌ وَرِثَ اَلْجَدَّ اَلْمَقْتُولَ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.5683 - Safwan ibn Yahya narrated from Ibn Abi Umayr, from Jamil, from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them), regarding a man who killed his father.
Imam (as) said: "He does not inherit from him. However, if the killer has a son, the son inherits from the murdered grandfather."
